title: Chromatic Grammar
description: A binding of semantic roles to 24-bit colors. One of several possible Grammars (Sonic, Spatial, etc.) defined by the Bone & Saucer / Semantic Visualization Language.

name.description: The proper name of this grammar (e.g. 'Moon Archive', 'Aggriparium').

etymology.description: Optional philological provenance for the grammar's name.

register.description: A short impressionistic tag for the grammar's mood (e.g. 'midnight · stone · archive').

palette.description: Mapping from Role name to color binding. Each key is a role; each value is a hex+gloss pair.

gloss.description: Short description of what this color *feels like* in this grammar.

reliqery.description: Reserved-empty slots. The Reliqery is the SVL's named silence: places the system knows belong here but has not yet filled.

slot.description: The name of the reserved slot.

expected_kind.description: What kind of thing will eventually fill this slot (e.g. 'Role', 'Grammar', 'Artifact', 'Expression').

kindred_grammars.description: Other Chromatic Grammars that share this role set and so may translate to/from this one losslessly.

the_archivist_says.description: A one-line aphorism sealing the grammar.
